items.headline: Rays beat Blue Jays 3-0 to complete a 3-game sweep
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Shane McClanahan extended his scoreless streak to 16 2/3 innings, and the Tampa Bay Rays beat the Toronto Blue Jays 3-0 Wednesday to finish a three-game sweep for their 12th win in 13 games.
Tampa Bay has allowed 17 runs over the 13 games. The Rays have three shutouts in that span and have given up one run five times.

items.headline: Yankees expect Rodón back Sunday, but Rice still sidelined with bruised hand
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Carlos Rodón is expected to rejoin the New York Yankees' rotation Sunday in Milwaukee, but first baseman Ben Rice remained out of the starting lineup for the third consecutive game Wednesday night with a bruised left hand.
Yankees manager Aaron Boone said it doesn't appear Rice will need to go on the injured list. The slugger got hurt catching a low pickoff throw during Sunday's win over Baltimore and left the game.

items.headline: Royals' Cole Ragans leaves start against Cleveland because of triceps and elbow soreness
items.description:
KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — In what Kansas City manager Matt Quatraro called “a no-brainer,” the Royals pulled left-hander Cole Ragans after three scoreless innings in his start against Cleveland on Wednesday night because of left triceps and elbow soreness.
“It’s routine for (pitching coach) Brian (Sweeney) to talk to the pitchers between every inning,” Quatraro said. “If somebody brings something up like that, it's a no brainer.”

items.headline: Reds closer Emilio Pagán placed on IL with left hamstring strain
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — Cincinnati Reds closer Emilio Pagán thought his season was over on Tuesday night.
A day later, it was a more positive timeline for the right-hander.

items.headline: Cubs win again after Matthew Boyd injures his left knee while sitting down with his kids at home
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Cubs are working on their plans with Matthew Boyd sidelined by a knee injury.
In the meantime, they won again.

items.headline: Tigers' Framber Valdez suspended 5 games by MLB for intentionally throwing at Boston's Trevor Story
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Detroit Tigers pitcher Framber Valdez was suspended for five games and fined on Wednesday, one day after he was ejected for hitting Boston's Trevor Story with a pitch during a 10-2 loss in which he allowed a career-high 10 runs.
Valdez was at first banned for six games by MLB, which cited him for intentionally throwing a pitch at Story, but the penalty was reduced in an agreement between Major League Baseball and the players' association. He started serving the penalty during Wednesday night's series finale and barring rainouts will be eligible to pitch Wednesday at the New York Mets.

items.headline: Blue Jays pitcher Max Scherzer to seek additional medical opinions on forearm
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Max Scherzer is not making progress in recovering from right forearm tendinitis and will seek additional medical opinions.
A 41-year-old right-hander, the three-time Cy Young Award winner was put on the injured list on April 27 because of the forearm injury and left ankle inflammation, a move retroactive to April 25. He had a light throwing session Wednesday before Toronto’s 3-0 loss to Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Dodgers’ Tyler Glasnow leaves vs Astros before second inning with back spasms
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers starter Tyler Glasnow left Wednesday’s game against the Houston Astros before the second inning because of back spasms but he doesn't believe the issue is serious.
Glasnow allowed a home run to leadoff hitter Brice Matthews before retiring the next three batters, with two strikeouts to end the first. He returned to the mound to warm up before the second inning but appeared to be in discomfort after throwing a pitch and signaled to the dugout.

items.headline: Astros star Carlos Correa faces season-ending surgery on a torn tendon in his ankle
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Houston’s Carlos Correa has a torn tendon in his left ankle that will require season-ending surgery, the star infielder said Wednesday.
Correa was injured Tuesday while taking swings in the batting cage before a game against the Los Angeles Dodgers.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Fred McGriff sets MLB record by homering in his 34th major league ballpark
items.description:
May 7
1917 — Babe Ruth of the Red Sox allowed two hits as he outdueled Walter Johnson of the Washington Senators 1-0. Ruth knocked in the winning run with a sacrifice fly.
